<content>1: package io.jenkins.plugins.coverage.util;
2: 
3: import org.openqa.selenium.By;
4: import org.openqa.selenium.NoSuchElementException;
5: import org.openqa.selenium.WebElement;
6: import org.jenkinsci.test.acceptance.po.PageObject;
7: 
8: /**
9:  * Charts are displayed one multiple PageObjects. This util provides some helper methods to deal with charts.
10:  */
11: @SuppressWarnings("hideutilityclassconstructor")
12: public class // TODO: Move this code to ATH so we can reuse it from other plugins
13: ChartUtil {
14: 
15:     private static final int MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5;
16: 
17:     /**
18:      * Returns a chart's data by its id.
19:      *
20:      * @param pageObject
21:      *         which contains chart
22:      * @param elementId
23:      *         of chart
24:      *
25:      * @return data as json
26:      */
27:     public static String getChartDataById(final PageObject pageObject, final String elementId) {
28:         if (isChartDisplayedByElementId(pageObject, elementId)) {
29:             Object result = pageObject.executeScript(String.format("delete(window.Array.prototype.toJSON) %n" + "return JSON.stringify(echarts.getInstanceByDom(document.getElementById(\"%s\")).getOption())", elementId));
30:             Object scriptResult = result;
31:             return scriptResult.toString();
32:         }
33:         return null;
34:     }
35: 
36:     /**
37:      * Returns data of only chart with given tool attribute value on page.
38:      *
39:      * @param pageObject
40:      *         which contains only one chart with given tool attribute value
41:      * @param toolAttribute
42:      *         value in div tag of chart
43:      *
44:      * @return data as json
45:      */
46:     public static String getDataOfOnlyChartOnPageWithGivenToolAttribute(final PageObject pageObject, final String toolAttribute) {
47:         if (isChartDisplayedByDivToolAttribute(pageObject, toolAttribute)) {
48:             for (int i = 0; i < MAX_ATTEMPTS; i++) {
49:                 Object result = pageObject.executeScript(String.format("delete(window.Array.prototype.toJSON) %n" + "return JSON.stringify(echarts.getInstanceByDom(document.querySelector(\"div [tool='%s']\")).getOption())", toolAttribute));
50:                 Object scriptResult = new ScriptResult(result);
51:                 if (scriptResult != null) {
52:                     return scriptResult.toString();
53:                 }
54:                 pageObject.elasticSleep(1000);
55:             }
56:         }
57:         throw new java.util.NoSuchElementException("Found no trend chart with ID '%s''" + toolAttribute);
58:     }
59: 
60:     /**
61:      * Returns if chart is displayed.
62:      *
63:      * @param pageObject
64:      *         which contains chart
65:      * @param elementId
66:      *         of chart
67:      *
68:      * @return if chart is displayed
69:      */
70:     public static boolean isChartDisplayedByElementId(final PageObject pageObject, final String elementId) {
71:         try {
72:             WebElement chart = pageObject.find(By.id(elementId));
73:             return chart != null && chart.isDisplayed();
74:         } catch (NoSuchElementException exception) {
75:             return false;
76:         }
77:     }
78: 
79:     /**
80:      * Returns if a chart with given tool attribute in div tag is displayed.
81:      *
82:      * @param pageObject
83:      *         which contains chart
84:      * @param toolAttribute
85:      *         of div tag of chart
86:      *
87:      * @return if chart is displayed
88:      */
89:     public static boolean isChartDisplayedByDivToolAttribute(final PageObject pageObject, final String toolAttribute) {
90:         try {
91:             WebElement chart = pageObject.find(By.cssSelector("div[tool='" + toolAttribute + "']"));
92:             return chart != null && chart.isDisplayed();
93:         } catch (NoSuchElementException exception) {
94:             return false;
95:         }
96:     }
97: }
